title: Clean Architecture
description: The goal of Clean Architecture is to make the business rules independent of frameworks, databases, UI, and external agencies.

title: Modular Monoliths
description: A modular monolith gives you 80% of the microservices benefits at 20% of the cost.

title: Monoliths vs Microservices
description: The monolith is not the enemy. Premature decomposition is.

title: gRPC Deep Dive
description: gRPC is what happens when Google decides HTTP + JSON is too slow for 10 billion internal RPCs per day.

title: Serialization Protocols
description: Serialization is the translation layer between in-memory objects and bytes on the wire (or disk).

title: Sync vs Async Communication
description: Blocking is not evil. But not knowing when to be non-blocking is.

title: Database Migrations
description: Use `apps.get_model()` in migrations — not direct model imports.

title: Django: Batteries-Included Web Framework
description: Learning path for Django project structure, MVT, ORM, views, DRF, and ecosystem patterns.

title: Data Shaping: Input vs Output Schemas
FastAPI: High-Performance Async APIs
description: Engineering guide for FastAPI routing, Pydantic validation, dependency injection, and async database access.

title: History and Evolution of Backend Systems
description: Understanding where we came from makes the decisions we make today legible.

title: The Perfect API Design
description: An API is a contract. Bad contracts create debt. Great contracts create ecosystems.

title: Welcome to the Machine: Client-Server Architecture
description: Every backend system is fundamentally a machine that listens, processes, and responds. This note maps the core model.

title: Backend Engineering Master Roadmap
description: A structured learning path for backend engineering covering frameworks, architecture patterns, communication protocols, databases, messaging, and observability.

title: Authentication & Authorization
description: Authentication answers "Who are you?" Authorization answers "What are you allowed to do?"

title: Rate Limiting & CORS
description: Two of the most commonly misconfigured — and misunderstood — web security mechanisms.

title: The Three Pillars of Observability
description: You cannot debug what you cannot see. Observability turns a black box into a glass box.

title: Declarative Transaction Management in Spring
description: @Transactional gives you declarative ACID boundaries. Understand proxies and propagation — or you'll debug "transaction not rolling back" at 2 AM.

title: Global Exception Handling in Spring Boot
description: Clients deserve **predictable error JSON**. Scatter `try/catch` in every controller and you'll ship inconsistent APIs. Use **`@ControllerAdvice`** once, handle everything centrally.

title: JPA, Hibernate, and Spring Data JDBC
description: **Spring Data JPA** hides boilerplate CRUD behind repositories. **Hibernate** is the default JPA provider — powerful, but you must understand lazy loading and the session.

title: Mastering Spring Core and IoC Containers
description: **Inversion of Control** means the framework creates and wires your objects — you describe *what* you need, not *how* to build the graph.

title: Properties, Profiles, and YAML Configuration
description: **Externalized configuration** lets you change behavior per environment without recompiling. Spring Boot loads config from files, env vars, and command-line args — with a clear precedence order.

title: REST Controllers and Spring MVC
description: Spring MVC maps HTTP requests to controller methods. **`@RestController`** = `@Controller` + `@ResponseBody` — every return value is serialized to JSON (by default).

title: Spring Boot Actuator and Metrics
description: You can't fix what you can't see. **Spring Boot Actuator** exposes health, metrics, and diagnostics — wire them to **Prometheus/Grafana** and protect them like production credentials.

title: Spring Boot Auto-Configuration Lifecycle
description: Spring Boot's magic is **conditional configuration** — beans appear only when the right classes and properties are on the classpath.

title: Spring Boot Testing and Slice Annotations
description: Not every test needs the full application context. **Slice tests** load only the layer you care about — faster CI, clearer failures.

title: Spring Boot: Enterprise Application Framework
description: Spring Boot learning path — IoC, configuration, JPA, web layer, security, testing, and observability.

title: Spring Security Architecture
description: Security in Spring is a **filter chain** in front of your servlets. You configure rules, authentication mechanisms, and method-level checks — the framework handles the rest.

title: PyTorch
description: Deep learning framework

title: Database & Persistence Knowledge Hub
description: Curated path from storage fundamentals to PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and production optimization.

title: Interacting with Databases: Raw Queries vs Query Builders vs ORMs
SQL vs NoSQL: Models, Tradeoffs, and When to Use Each
description: There is no universal winner—only fit for your consistency, scale, and query patterns.

title: What Is a Database? History and Evolution
description: A database is an **organized** collection of structured information, stored and managed by a **Database Management System (DBMS)**.

title: MongoDB Architecture and Schema Design
description: Deep dive placeholder—flexible schemas, replica sets, sharding, and aggregation for analytics.

title: PostgreSQL Architecture and Internals
description: Deep dive placeholder—processes, shared buffers, WAL, B-tree vs GiST indexes, and EXPLAIN ANALYZE workflows.

title: Redis Architecture: Why Single-Threaded is Ultra-Fast
EC2
description: 400 Gbps Ethernet networking max performance gap

title: 1. What is it and what is it for?
description: Docker is software plattform that allows create, deploy and execute application inside of containers. Basically, it's the tool that made the classic programmer phrase, "But it worked on my computer," obsolete.

title: DevOps & Cloud Master Roadmap
description: A structured learning path for DevOps and Cloud engineering covering containers, cloud providers, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure automation.

title: Computing Master Index
description: Navigable map of core computing concepts (hardware, boot, networking, storage, operating systems)

title: DNS
description: Domain Name System — distributed name resolution, resolver chain, record types, and security considerations.

title: gRPC
description: High-performance RPC framework over HTTP/2 — Protocol Buffers, streaming, and when to choose gRPC over REST.

title: How the Internet Works
description: End-to-end mental map — from user input to bits on the wire, through ISPs, DNS, IP routing, encryption, and CDNs.

title: HTTP
description: Hypertext Transfer Protocol — request/response semantics, methods, headers, status codes, and HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2.

title: HTTPS
description: HTTP secured with TLS — encryption on the wire, certificates, port 443, and HSTS.

title: IP (Internet Protocol)
description: Internet Protocol — datagram delivery, headers, IPv4 vs IPv6, and its role in the network layer.

title: IP Addresses and Protocols
description: IPv4 and IPv6 address formats, public vs private space, loopback, and how IANA/RIRs allocate addresses.

title: NAT
description: Network Address Translation — sharing public IPv4, NAT tables, port forwarding, and developer implications.

title: Network Protocols Index
description: Categorized index of protocol notes in this vault, organized by OSI-style layers.

title: Networking: Fundamentals & Protocols
description: Learning path from how the Internet works through the OSI model to individual protocol deep dives.

title: OSI Model
description: The seven-layer OSI reference model — what each layer does and how TCP/IP protocols map to it.

title: TCP
description: Transmission Control Protocol — connection-oriented, reliable, ordered byte streams over IP.

title: TLS
description: Transport Layer Security — handshake, certificates, symmetric encryption, and TLS 1.3 essentials.

title: UDP
description: User Datagram Protocol — connectionless transport, trade-offs vs TCP, and common use cases.

title: WebRTC
description: Browser real-time communication — peer connections, ICE/STUN/TURN, media over UDP, and data channels.

title: ACID
description: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — transactional guarantees in RDBMS.

title: Analytics & Data Platforms
description: Large-scale analytics storage — data lakes vs warehouses.

title: B-Tree
description: Balanced tree index — default for most relational databases.

title: Backpressure
description: Slow producers when consumers are overloaded.

title: BASE
description: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent — pragmatic alternative to ACID at scale.

title: Bloom Filter
description: Probabilistic structure: quickly tells you a key is **definitely not** present.

title: Caching
description: Store frequently accessed data in faster tier (memory, CDN).

title: CAP Theorem
description: Under partition, a system must choose between consistency and availability.

title: Change Data Capture
description: CDC — stream database changes to downstream systems in real time.

title: Checkpointing
description: Flushing dirty pages to disk so recovery replays less WAL.

title: Circuit Breaker
description: Stop calling a failing dependency after threshold — fail fast.

title: Clustering
description: Physically ordering or grouping related rows on disk or nodes.

title: Columnar Storage
description: Store each column contiguously — analytics optimized.

title: Compaction
description: Merging SSTables or reclaiming space in LSM and append-only stores.

title: Composite Index
description: Single index spanning multiple columns in defined order.

title: Concurrency Control & Locking
description: How databases serialize concurrent access — deadlocks and lock strategies.

title: Connection Pooling
description: Reuse DB connections instead of opening per request.

title: Consensus
description: Agreement among nodes on a single value or log order despite failures.

title: Consistency Models
description: Guarantees readers see — ACID, eventual vs strong, snapshots, and MVCC.

title: Consistent Hashing
description: Hash ring mapping keys to nodes with minimal remapping when nodes change.

title: Cost-Based Optimizer
description: Planner that picks the lowest-estimated-cost plan using table statistics.

title: Covering Index
description: Index that contains all columns needed by a query — no heap fetch.

title: Data Integration & Change Propagation
description: Moving and syncing data — CDC, migration, idempotency, exactly-once.

title: Data Lake
description: Central repository for raw structured and unstructured data at scale.

title: Data Locality
description: Keeping compute close to the data it processes to cut network cost.

title: Data Migration
description: Moving data between schemas, engines, or regions.

title: Data Modeling & Schema Design
description: How to structure data — normalization, evolution, and precomputed views.

title: Data Skew
description: Uneven data or query distribution across partitions or workers.

title: Data Warehouse
description: Structured, curated store optimized for SQL analytics and BI.

title: Deadlock
description: Circular wait — each transaction holds a lock the other needs.

title: Denormalization
description: Intentionally duplicating data to speed reads at the cost of write complexity.

title: Dirty Read
description: Reading uncommitted data from another transaction.

title: Distributed Systems Fundamentals
description: Core ideas for multiple nodes — consensus, quorums, CAP, and split-brain.

title: Distributed Transactions
description: Coordinating commits across multiple nodes — 2PC and 3PC.

title: Eventual Consistency
description: Replicas converge over time if no new writes — no immediate global truth.

title: Exactly-Once Semantics
description: Each message/effect processed once despite retries and failures.

title: Failover
description: Automatic or manual switch to standby when primary fails.

title: Federation
description: Splitting databases by domain or tenant across independent systems.

title: General Concept Glossary (legacy index)
description: Legacy flat list — see 00_Software_Engineer_MOC for the organized concept library.

title: Geo-Replication
description: Replicating data across geographic regions for latency and DR.

title: Hash Partitioning
description: Assign rows to partitions via hash(partition_key) mod N.

title: High Availability
description: HA — designing for continued service through component failure.

title: Horizontal Scaling
description: Add more machines/nodes to handle load — scale out.

title: Hot Partition
description: One shard receiving disproportionate traffic — bottleneck for the whole system.

title: Idempotency
description: Same operation applied multiple times has the same effect as once.

title: Indexing
description: Auxiliary data structures that let the engine find rows without full table scans.

title: Indexing & Query Optimization
description: How indexes and planners make queries fast — B-trees, LSM, and bloom filters.

title: Leader-Follower Replication
description: Single leader accepts writes; followers replicate the log.

title: Load Balancing
description: Distribute requests across healthy backends.

title: Lock Escalation
description: Engine upgrades many row locks to table lock to save memory.

title: Logical Replication
description: Replicate row/change events (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) at logical level.

title: LSM Tree
description: Log-Structured Merge tree — write-optimized storage used in Cassandra, RocksDB, LevelDB.

title: Materialized Views
description: Persisted query results refreshed on a schedule or on change.

title: Multi-Leader Replication
description: Multiple nodes accept writes — conflicts possible.

title: MVCC
description: Multi-Version Concurrency Control — keep old row versions for concurrent readers.

title: Normalization
description: Organizing data into tables to reduce redundancy and update anomalies.

title: Optimistic Locking
description: Assume no conflict; check version at commit time.

title: Partitioning, Sharding & Data Distribution
description: Splitting data across nodes — hash/range keys, hot spots, and rebalancing.

title: Pessimistic Locking
description: Acquire locks before modifying — SELECT FOR UPDATE.

title: Phantom Read
description: Same query returns different rows because another txn inserted/deleted.

title: Physical Replication
description: Copy WAL/storage pages block-by-block to standby.

title: Query Planner
description: Engine component that chooses execution strategy for a SQL query.

title: Quorum
description: Minimum number of nodes that must agree for a read or write to proceed.

title: Range Partitioning
description: Split data by contiguous key ranges (dates, IDs).

title: Rate Limiting
description: Cap requests per client/IP/key in a time window.

title: Read Replicas
description: Secondary copies that serve read traffic while leader handles writes.

title: Read Skew
description: Reading two related rows at different points in time — inconsistent snapshot.

title: Rebalancing
description: Moving partitions or hash ranges between nodes to equalize load.

title: Replication & Data Distribution
description: Copies of data across nodes — leader/follower, geo-replication, and federation.

title: Resharding
description: Changing the number of shards or partition scheme.

title: Resilience, Reliability & Availability
description: Staying up under failure — failover, HA, circuit breakers, and throttling.

title: Row-Based Storage
description: Store all columns of a row together — OLTP default.

title: Scalability & Performance
description: Growing systems — horizontal vs vertical scale, pooling, cache, backpressure.

title: Schema Evolution
description: Changing database schema over time without breaking running applications.

title: Schema Registry
description: Central store for data schemas (often Avro/Protobuf/JSON Schema) in streaming pipelines.

title: Secondary Indexes
description: Indexes on non-primary-key columns for alternate lookup paths.

title: Snapshot Isolation
description: Each transaction reads from a consistent snapshot as of transaction start.

title: Software Engineer: Data & Systems Concepts
description: Master index of 73 core concepts for backend and data engineering — modeling, indexing, distribution, consistency, and operations.

title: Split-Brain
description: Two partitions both believe they are the leader — risk of divergent writes.

title: Storage Engine Internals
description: How data hits disk — WAL, compaction, row vs columnar layouts.

title: Strong Consistency
description: After a write completes, all readers see the new value.

title: Three-Phase Commit
description: 3PC — adds pre-commit phase to reduce blocking vs 2PC.

title: Throttling
description: Gradually slow request rate to protect system capacity.

title: Time-Series Partitioning
description: Range partitioning by time — common for metrics and logs.

title: Transaction Anomalies
description: Isolation failures — dirty reads, phantoms, and skew patterns.

title: Two-Phase Commit
description: 2PC — coordinator prepares then commits all participants atomically.

title: Vertical Scaling
description: Bigger CPU/RAM/disk on one machine — scale up.

title: Write Skew
description: Two transactions read overlapping state and write disjoint rows — invariant breaks.

title: Write-Ahead Logging
description: WAL — log changes before applying to data pages for crash recovery.

title: ConfigMaps and Secrets
description: Decouple configuration and sensitive values from container images using ConfigMaps and Secrets.

title: Declarative vs Imperative in Kubernetes
description: YAML manifests and GitOps-style declarative workflows versus imperative kubectl commands.

title: Deployments and Rollout Strategies
description: Deployments, rolling updates, recreate strategy, and rolling back container image versions.

title: Ingress and Ingress Controllers
description: HTTP routing into the cluster with Ingress resources and controllers such as NGINX and Traefik.

title: Kubernetes Control Plane Components
description: How the API Server, etcd, Scheduler, and Controller Manager coordinate cluster state.

title: Kubernetes Master Roadmap
description: Home hub for Kubernetes: learning path, kubectl cheat sheet, glossary, and links from Docker containers to cluster orchestration.

title: Kubernetes Probes: Liveness, Readiness and Startup
description: Configure Liveness, Readiness, and Startup probes for self-healing and zero-downtime deployments of containerized backends.

title: Namespaces and RBAC
description: Logical cluster isolation with namespaces and role-based access control for teams and services.

title: Persistent Volumes, Claims and StorageClasses
description: PV, PVC, and StorageClass for durable storage decoupled from Pod lifecycle.

title: Pod Networking and CNI
description: The IP-per-Pod model, CNI plugins, and how cluster networking relates to container network namespaces.

title: Pods and Lifecycle
description: The Pod as the smallest deployable unit, container states, restart policies, and init containers.

title: ReplicaSets
description: How ReplicaSets maintain a stable set of Pod replicas and enable self-healing.

title: Resource Requests, Limits and OOM
description: CPU and memory requests/limits, QoS classes, and handling OOMKilled containers.

title: Services: ClusterIP, NodePort and LoadBalancer
description: Kubernetes Service types for stable cluster networking and exposing workloads inside and outside the cluster.

title: StatefulSets, Jobs and CronJobs
description: Stateful workloads with stable identity and batch Jobs/CronJobs for one-off or scheduled tasks.

title: Volumes and Mounts
description: Ephemeral and shared storage inside Pods: emptyDir, hostPath, and volume mounts.

title: Worker Nodes: Kubelet, kube-proxy and Container Runtime
description: Anatomy of worker nodes: kubelet, kube-proxy, and the CRI runtime that runs OCI images from Docker-style builds.

title: C#: Fundamentals and .NET Essentials
description: Beginner-to-intermediate path for C#—types, OOP, collections, LINQ, exceptions, and the .NET CLI.

title: LINQ Basics
description: **LINQ** (Language Integrated Query) queries collections with a SQL-like fluent API.

title: Nullability and Type Safety
description: **Nullable reference types** (NRT) help document whether `string` can be `null`—the compiler warns you when you forget checks.

title: Properties and Encapsulation
description: **Properties** are C#'s idiomatic way to expose data with optional validation—prefer them over public fields.

title: Types and Variables in C#
description: C# is **statically typed**—the compiler knows types at build time. That catches many bugs before runtime.

title: Channels and Buffering
description: **Don't communicate by sharing memory; share memory by communicating.** Channels are typed conduits for sending values between goroutines.

title: Garbage Collector Tuning
description: Go uses a **non-generational, concurrent, tri-color mark-and-sweep** collector—designed for low pause times in server workloads.

title: Gin Framework Architecture
description: **Gin** is a high-performance HTTP web framework built on `httprouter`—minimal API, middleware chains, JSON binding.

title: Go Modules and Tooling
description: Modules are the unit of dependency management—`go.mod` declares your module path and requirements.

title: Go: Simplicity, Concurrency, and Systems Engineering
description: Master roadmap for Go—from pointers and composition to goroutines, the GMP scheduler, GC tuning, and production APIs with Gin.

title: Goroutines vs OS Threads
description: Goroutines are lightweight, multiplexed onto fewer OS threads by the Go scheduler—not a 1:1 mapping.

title: Idiomatic Error Handling in Go
description: Errors are **values**, not exceptions. Control flow is explicit: `if err != nil`.

title: Implicit Interfaces and Duck Typing
description: If it walks like a duck and quacks like a duck—Go doesn't need an `implements` keyword. Satisfaction is **implicit**.

title: Memory Allocation and Escape Analysis
description: The compiler decides **stack** vs **heap** allocation via **escape analysis**—not every `new` hits the GC.

title: Pointers and Memory: Value vs Reference
description: When to pass by value vs by pointer—and how Go keeps memory semantics explicit without manual malloc/free.

title: Slices and Arrays: Internals
description: Arrays are fixed-size; slices are the dynamic view everyone uses daily. Understanding the slice header prevents subtle bugs.

title: Structs, Embedding, and Composition
description: Go has **no inheritance**. Behavior and data are composed with structs, embedding, and interfaces.

title: sync Package: WaitGroup, Mutex, and When to Use What
description: Channels orchestrate; `sync` primitives protect shared memory. Know when to use each.

title: The Go Scheduler: G-M-P Model
description: **G**oroutine, **M**achine (OS thread), **P**rocessor (logical CPU context)—the runtime multiplexes many Gs onto fewer Ms.

title: The Select Statement
description: `select` waits on multiple channel operations—Go's answer to multiplexed I/O and timeouts.
